findings.title: Race condition in stream retry counter
findings.body:
The retry counter is read outside the mutex while concurrent goroutines increment it.

Under load the counter can skip values, so `maxRetries` is never reached and the loop retries forever:

```go
for attempt := 0; attempt < c.maxRetries; attempt++ {
    go c.retry(attempt) // attempt captured racily
}
```

Wrap the read in the same mutex or use `atomic.Int64`.

suggestions.body:
Alternatively hold `c.mu` across the read:
```go
c.mu.Lock()
n := c.retries
c.mu.Unlock()
```

findings.title: Missing error check on file close
findings.body: `defer f.Close()` discards the error on a file opened for writing; buffered data may be lost silently.

overall_explanation: The change introduces a **data race** in the retry path and one minor resource-handling issue. The race is reachable in production whenever more than one stream retries concurrently.
